use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use openssl because Secure Sockets Layer toolkit - cryptographic utility.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use openssl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `openssl command [ options ... ] [ parameters ... ]` as documented upstream for openssl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `openssl no-XXX [ options ]` as documented upstream for openssl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `openssl -help | -version` as documented upstream for openssl.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair openssl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `openssl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install openssl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `openssl command [ options ... ] [ parameters ... ]`. Further invocations from upstream: `openssl -help | -version`. Secure Sockets Layer toolkit - cryptographic utility.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Secure Sockets Layer toolkit - cryptographic utility.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Secure Sockets Layer toolkit - cryptographic utility.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install openssl
Install with: sudo apt install openssl
